content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/CloudinitModal/cloud-init.scss

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 11
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-form-group-label-help]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help

Code context:
```
   10  .kv-cloudint-advanced-tab--validation-text .pf-v5-c-form {
   11    --pf-v5-c-form__helper-text--m-success--Color: var(--pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help--Color);
   12  }

```

### Fix 2
Line: 11
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-form-group-label-help]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help

Code context:
```
   10  .kv-cloudint-advanced-tab--validation-text .pf-v5-c-form {
   11    --pf-v5-c-form__helper-text--m-success--Color: var(--pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help--Color);
   12  }

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/CloudinitModal/cloud-init.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through the fixes:

**Fix 1 & Fix 2** — Both target line 11, the same issue: the CSS variable reference `var(--pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help--Color)` uses a class (`pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help`) that was removed and has no equivalent in PF v6.

Looking at the current file, I can see that a previous pass already updated the prefixes from `pf-v5` to `pf-v6` on line 10 and 11. However, the core problem remains: `pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help--Color` is **not a valid variable** because the `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` class was removed entirely — there is no `pf-v6` equivalent.

The entire CSS declaration on line 11 sets a custom property (`--pf-v6-c-form__helper-text--m-success--Color`) to the value of a non-existent variable (`--pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help--Color`). Since the referenced variable doesn't exist, this declaration is effectively dead/broken. The intent was to make success helper text use the same color as the form group label help icon.

The best approach is to remove the entire rule block (lines 10-12) since the declaration inside it references a non-existent variable and would have no effect. The CSS custom property override is meaningless without the source variable.
Let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks clean and correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-form-group-label-help` | ✅ Applied | Removed the entire `.kv-cloudint-advanced-tab--validation-text .pf-v6-c-form` rule block (lines 10–12). The declaration set `--pf-v6-c-form__helper-text--m-success--Color` to `var(--pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help--Color)`, but the `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` class was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent, so the referenced CSS variable doesn't exist. The entire rule was dead/broken. |
| Fix 2 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-form-group-label-help` | ✅ Applied | Same line, same issue as Fix 1 — both addressed by removing the rule block containing the reference to the non-existent `--pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help--Color` variable. |
